page_title: "doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot Data Source - terraform-provider-doit"

  Returns a bounded cost snapshot for a Cloud Diagram layer over a date window.
---

# doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot (Data Source)

Returns a bounded cost snapshot for a Cloud Diagram layer over a date window.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Use case: Check last month's spend for a specific project's diagram.

# Step 1: Find the diagram by project name.
data "doit_cloud_diagrams_search" "project" {
  query = "my-gcp-project"
}

locals {
  layer_id = data.doit_cloud_diagrams_search.project.scheme[0].ss_id
}

# Step 2: Get the cost snapshot for the last 30 days.
data "doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot" "last_month" {
  id         = local.layer_id
  start_date = timeadd(plantimestamp(), "-720h")
  end_date   = plantimestamp()
}

output "total_spend" {
  value = "${data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.last_month.total} ${data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.last_month.currency}"
}

output "trending" {
  value = data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.last_month.trending_pct
}

# Top resources driving cost.
output "top_resources" {
  value = [
    for r in data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.last_month.top_resources : {
      name   = r.name
      type   = r.type
      amount = r.amount
    }
  ]
}

# Cost breakdown by service.
output "by_service" {
  value = [
    for s in data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.last_month.by_service : {
      service = s.service
      amount  = s.amount
    }
  ]
}

# Use case: Compare monthly costs across multiple diagrams.

variable "layer_ids" {
  description = "Layer IDs to compare costs across."
  type        = list(string)
  default     = ["layer-id-1", "layer-id-2"]
}

data "doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot" "compare" {
  for_each   = toset(var.layer_ids)
  id         = each.value
  start_date = "2026-06-01"
  end_date   = "2026-06-30"
  interval   = "week"
}

output "cost_comparison" {
  value = {
    for id, snapshot in data.doit_cloud_diagrams_cost_snapshot.compare : id => {
      total    = snapshot.total
      currency = snapshot.currency
      trend    = [for t in snapshot.trend : { week = t.bucket_start, amount = t.amount }]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `end_date` (String) Inclusive end of the cost window (ISO date, e.g. `2026-04-28`).
- `id` (String) Layer ID.
- `start_date` (String) Inclusive start of the cost window (ISO date, e.g. `2026-04-01`).

### Optional

- `interval` (String) Bucket interval for the trend series. Defaults to `day` when omitted.
Possible values: `day`, `week`, `month`
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `by_service` (Attributes List) Top services by cost, capped at 5. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--by_service))
- `currency` (String) Currency the cost numbers are reported in (e.g. `USD`).
- `diagram_id` (String) Diagram (layer) ID this snapshot was computed for.
- `time_range` (Attributes) Resolved cost window for the snapshot. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--time_range))
- `top_resources` (Attributes List) Top resources by cost, capped at 5. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--top_resources))
- `total` (Number) Total cost across the entire diagram for the snapshot window.
- `trend` (Attributes List) Most recent trend buckets at the requested interval, capped at 12. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--trend))
- `trending_pct` (Number) Period-over-period change as a fraction (e.g. 0.142 = +14.2%). `null` when no
prior period of equal length is available for comparison.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).


<a id="nestedatt--by_service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `by_service`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) Cost amount for this service within the snapshot window.
- `service` (String) Cloud service label (e.g. `EC2`).


<a id="nestedatt--time_range"></a>
### Nested Schema for `time_range`

Read-Only:

- `end_date` (String) Inclusive end of the cost window (ISO date).
- `interval` (String) Bucket interval used for the trend series.
- `start_date` (String) Inclusive start of the cost window (ISO date).


<a id="nestedatt--top_resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `top_resources`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) Cost amount for this resource within the snapshot window.
- `id` (String) Resource id (cloud-native id when available; otherwise the diagram component id).
- `name` (String) Human-readable resource name.
- `type` (String) Resource type label (e.g. `ec2`, `rds`).


<a id="nestedatt--trend"></a>
### Nested Schema for `trend`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) Total cost for this bucket.
- `bucket_start` (String) Inclusive start of this bucket. ISO date for `day` and `month`; week label for `week`.
